title: Document crates.io account setup for cargo publish
description: 03-real-world-skills/05-shipping-it.md:289 says 'You will need a crates.io account linked to your GitHub. The first time you run cargo publish, it will walk you through the authentication.' That is not enough for a novice. The actual steps: (1) sign in to crates.io with GitHub OAuth, (2) confirm email address from the email crates.io sends (must be verified before publishing), (3) go to Account Settings > API Tokens > New Token, (4) run 'cargo login <token>' which stores it in ~/.cargo/credentials.toml, (5) then 'cargo publish' works. Also important context a novice will not know: crate names are first-come-first-served globally, you cannot unpublish a version (only yank), version numbers must increment, published crates are public forever. Fix: Expand the crates.io section in 03-real-world-skills/05-shipping-it.md with the numbered walkthrough and the 'forever public' warning. Add a note that publishing is optional for the curriculum and most apprentice projects should stay unpublished.
